entries.author: agent:portfolio_geometry_d

entries.author: agent:jc_sp_stage4_probe

entries.novelty_witness: compound regularity and bound => exact scale probes isolate the missing C3 full-cell ledger; novelty=reformulation
entries.invalidated_inference: one may estimate D3 A_N before proving that every terminal complement endpoint ground-state metric gauge area base and lower-row descendant belongs to one common C3 scalar identity

entries.author: agent:qrci_stage4_probe

entries.novelty_witness: form-level C3 plausibility => boundary trace and corner coefficient differentiation remains a distinct unproved map; novelty=reformulation
entries.invalidated_inference: C3 dependence in H_0^1 automatically permits three termwise derivatives of reduced-DtN conormal trace corner coefficient and endpoint distribution expressions

entries.author: agent:codex_stage5_strategy
entries.mechanism: replace the false product-edge and one-family boundary interface by compatible global trace spaces and the two completed-scalar polygon families
entries.novelty_witness: Stage 4 model integrability => exact Stage 5 type check refutes the lifting and coefficient conventions; novelty=invariant
entries.invalidated_inference: arbitrary product side H1/2 data admit a global H1 lifting and one polygon family canonically supplies common/jump corner channels
entries.reduction_witness: JC_SP_REG => typed two-family material regularity JC_SP_MATREG; axis=regularity
entries.next_decision_test: if JC_SP_MATREG passes typed trace and corner-map audit => continue-attack; else => route-expansion
entries.summary: JC_SP_REG used a nonexistent right inverse on arbitrary product-edge H1/2 data, applied form operators outside their declared domain, and defined two common/jump channels from only one polygon family.

entries.author: agent:matreg_counterexample_scout
entries.mechanism: type scalar and weak material conormal pullbacks separately and index endpoint coefficients by vertex incidence and common/jump channel
entries.novelty_witness: compatible global trace repair => exact side-coordinate pullback exposes distinct rho^(beta-1) and rho^beta coefficients; novelty=invariant
entries.invalidated_inference: the physical coefficient beta*c is unchanged under fixed-side material pullback and one endpoint coefficient per vertex suffices
entries.next_decision_test: if the corrected scalar/flux convention passes Stage 4 => continue-attack; else => route-expansion
entries.summary: JC_SP_MATREG omitted the side-length-ratio factors distinguishing scalar conormal pullback from weak flux-density pullback and allocated too few endpoint channels.

entries.author: agent:matflux_proof_auditor_1
entries.mechanism: rerun the eigenpair IFT under a sector-compatible fixed-interface material map and use factored Bessel-ratio bounds for all modes
entries.novelty_witness: failed moving-H1 composition and unsummed Parseval step => independent sector materialization plus exponential all-mode ratio estimate; novelty=construction
entries.invalidated_inference: C3 H1 dependence under one piecewise-affine materialization survives arbitrary moving-sector composition and Parseval alone controls three order derivatives
entries.reduction_witness: JC_SP_MATFLUX O5-O6 => analytic support path plus second material-map IFT plus factored Bessel ratio summation; axis=regularity
entries.next_decision_test: if repaired packet passes two independent line audits => continue-attack; else => route-expansion
entries.summary: The first JC_SP_MATFLUX proof used a false H1 moving-composition inference, omitted analytic support-path dependence, and lacked differentiated all-mode Bessel H1/2 summation.
